rationale_for_baseline_tier: After a negative UGIB/ACS screen and a classic low-risk panic picture, the safe course is de-escalation with prompt GP follow-up (B12/gastritis and anxiety) plus a firm UGIB/ACS safety-net — a T2 baseline. Emergency escalation is over-triage; unscreened dismissal under-triages the small residual bleed/cardiac risk.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting blood/coffee-grounds, black tarry stools, fainting, or crushing/exertional chest pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate acute GI bleeding or ACS requiring emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you vomit blood or coffee-ground material, pass black tarry stools, faint, or get crushing chest pain spreading to your arm/jaw, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Family can support and monitor while she settles.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a panic response, not internal bleeding or cancer. Do slow breathing and grounding, close the research tabs, and see your GP within a week about the B12/gastritis finding. If you vomit blood or coffee-ground material, pass black stools, faint, or get crushing chest pain, get emergency help.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; 000/ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight for a red-flag symptom; otherwise this can wait for the GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a panic attack triggered by an incidental autoimmune-gastritis result; UGIB/ACS screened negative; paced breathing/grounding and safety-net given; routine B12/gastritis follow-up advised.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing as anxiety with no UGIB/ACS screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An occult UGIB or atypical ACS is mislabelled panic and the patient deteriorates unmonitored.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable if it carries the UGIB/ACS safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a clearly low-risk panic attack is over-triage and reinforces catastrophic beliefs.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine, obtain Hb/ECG/troponin, or endoscope remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use a structured low-risk assessment to de-escalate with an explicit UGIB/ACS safety-net; escalate on any red flag.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismiss without screening or dispatch an ambulance reflexively.
